title: Malta Proposes DeFi Rulebook Covering DAOs Under MiCA Framework
summary: Malta's regulator is seeking feedback on a legal framework for decentralized finance (DeFi) and decentralized autonomous organizations (DAOs), arguing that many DeFi projects are not fully decentralized. This move aims to bring more clarity to the rapidly evolving DeFi space under the MiCA (Markets in Crypto-Assets) regulations.
body:
Malta's financial regulator is proposing a new legal framework for DeFi and DAOs, seeking industry feedback. The regulator argues that many DeFi projects are not fully decentralized, making them subject to regulatory oversight. This move aligns with the broader MiCA regulations, which aim to provide a comprehensive regulatory framework for crypto assets in the European Union.

The proposed framework will cover software-governed organizations, including DAOs, and will define the responsibilities and liabilities of those involved in these projects. The regulator is particularly focused on ensuring that DeFi projects comply with anti-money laundering (AML) and counter-terrorism financing (CTF) regulations. This initiative is part of Malta's ongoing efforts to establish itself as a leading hub for blockchain and crypto innovation.

For everyday users, this development could mean greater transparency and security in the DeFi space. As regulations become clearer, users may feel more confident participating in DeFi projects, knowing that there are established rules to protect them. However, it could also mean increased scrutiny and potential restrictions on certain DeFi activities.

The regulator is currently seeking feedback from the industry, with a deadline for submissions expected soon. This is a critical period for stakeholders to voice their opinions and shape the future of DeFi regulation in Malta. Users and investors should watch for updates on the finalized framework, as it will have significant implications for the DeFi ecosystem.

The proposed framework will define responsibilities and liabilities for DeFi projects, focusing on AML and CTF compliance. Malta aims to establish itself as a leading hub for blockchain innovation.

This development could mean greater transparency and security for DeFi users. Watch for updates on the finalized framework, as it will shape the future of DeFi in Malta. Read more → https://www.cryptocatalyst.news/articles/2026-06-18-malta-proposes-defi-rulebook-covering-daos-under-mica-framework
